title: Did DeChambeau attempt to involve Trump in Open rules controversy?
extracted_text:
The Open: Did Bryson DeChambeau attempt to involve President Donald Trump during rules controversy at Royal Birkdale?
Bryson DeChambeau was handed a two-shot penalty for a rules breach during the second round of The Open; multiple media reports have claimed DeChambeau wanted to involve President Donald Trump as he tried to contest the decision at Royal Birkdale
Monday 20 July 2026 17:00, UK
Bryson DeChambeau has been accused of wanting to involve United States President Donald Trump during his rules controversy at The Open, multiple media reports have claimed.
The two-time major winner was handed a two-shot penalty late on Friday night after officials reviewed footage and decided he had improved the area of his backswing by flattening down some long grass on Royal Birkdale's fifth hole.
Multiple reports claim DeChambeau allegedly threatened to call Trump during lengthy discussions with the R&A regarding the penalty, which dropped him from second place and one off the lead on Friday to tied-fifth and three back.
- Bryson DeChambeau's rules drama explained
- Rory McIlroy criticises 'performative' DeChambeau
- Scottie Scheffler defends DeChambeau over ruling
- Get Sky Sports or stream golf with no contract
Sky Sports News has contacted the R&A, the White House press office and DeChambeau's management team for comment.
DeChambeau is a friend of Trump, serving on the President's council on sports, fitness and nutrition, making his last White House appearance in May, and has played golf with him.
Trump asked football's world governing body FIFA to review the red card shown to US forward Folarin Balogun during the World Cup, with the player's one-match ban subsequently being suspended, and DeChambeau wanted a similar intervention.
While the call did not take place at the time, it is not known whether DeChambeau made contact privately, but on Saturday R&A chief executive Mark Darbon was asked whether the President had been in touch.
